human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for facilities-specific analytics boundaries, metric-definition rigor, permit and inspection gating, work-order and benchmark evidence handling, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to facilities analytics, metric-definition logic, reconciliation notes, benchmark caveats, work-order evidence, and disclosure-safe escalation rather than generic BI work.

Authority sources cover EPA benchmarking and metric-definition guidance, GSA and DOE work-order and CMMS baselines, OSHA safety gating, AHJ permit and inspection guidance, WBDG closeout references, and SEC disclosure discipline.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope analysis, ambiguous metrics, stale or incomplete data, conflicting systems, unsupported causal claims, permit blockers, inspection blockers, authority refusal, changed safety posture, boundary rejection, missing facts, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/facilities-services-and-building-operations/facilities-analytics-specialist/ so it remains distinct from adjacent project controls, permitting, inspection, safety, superintendent, and licensed-trade lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated facilities analytics specialist for metric-definition support, work-order and benchmark reporting, source reconciliation, caveat design, and safe escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
